## Overview

**v0.3.1 — macOS emoji picker polish.**

A small patch on top of v0.3.0 that improves the macOS emoji picker
ergonomics and documents two limitations encountered during macOS demos.
No code changes for Windows; the Windows binary is rebuilt from the same
sources only to keep the per-platform release artifacts in lockstep.

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows | `tildaz-v0.3.1-windows.zip` | unzip anywhere. Includes `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY runtime. |
| macOS (universal) | `tildaz-v0.3.1-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel single binary. Drag `TildaZ.app` into Applications. macOS 13 Ventura or later. |

First-launch on macOS: the app is ad-hoc signed, so macOS may block the
first open. Right-click `TildaZ.app` → Open, or run
`xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/TildaZ.app`. Then grant
Accessibility + Input Monitoring permissions once.

## What's new since v0.3.0

### macOS — Emoji & Symbols picker

- **Press `Esc` to dismiss the picker.** Previously you had to hit
  `Ctrl+Cmd+Space` again to toggle it off. The dismiss path queries the
  picker's actual visibility via `CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo` (matching
  any process whose `bundleIdentifier` starts with `com.apple.Character`),
  so it stays accurate even if the picker is closed by some other route
  ([#130](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/130)).
- **Removed the redundant `Ctrl+Shift+Space` menu item.** That shortcut
  isn't an Apple default in any macOS version we could verify; it had
  been hardcoded based on a mistaken assumption. The Edit menu now has a
  single "Emoji & Symbols" entry bound to the actual default,
  `Ctrl+Cmd+Space`.
- **Documented the known UX gap** vs. Terminal.app: the picker shows as
  a free-floating window rather than a cursor-anchored popover, and
  macOS doesn't auto-dismiss it on focus loss. Apple reserves the
  popover path for `NSTextView`-based first responders. Every modern
  fast terminal on macOS — Ghostty, iTerm2, Alacritty, Kitty — shares
  this limitation; the cell-grid + GPU-atlas + custom-IME architecture
  they all use is fundamentally incompatible with `NSTextView`.

### Documentation

- **Multi-paste limitation under bash 3.2** documented
  ([#141](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/141)). Holding `Cmd+V`
  to flood-paste a wide grapheme cluster like `👨‍👩‍👧` stalls line wrap on
  bash 3.2 because ghostty's Mode 2027 (grapheme clustering) treats the
  cluster as 2 cells, while bash 3.2's `wcwidth(3)` is cluster-unaware
  and reserves the codepoint-sum width (6 cells per family). The 4-cell
  per-family mismatch eventually pushes bash's internal cursor model
  past its wrap threshold, and bash redraws the line in place. Single
  pastes are unaffected; the issue only appears under sustained key
  repeat. The fixes available either break the family-emoji ligature
  outright or require forking ghostty's grid model, so this is now a
  documented known limitation. macOS default shell is migrating to zsh
  5.x, whose line editor is cluster-aware and doesn't exhibit the
  mismatch.

## Known issues

- **[#141](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/141)** — see above.
  Holding `Cmd+V` on a wide grapheme cluster under bash 3.2 stalls line
  wrap; documented limitation, no fix planned.
- **[#144](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/144)** — at high
  zoom, anti-aliasing edges and color-channel transitions are still
  microscopically rougher than Windows Terminal. Not a release blocker.
- **[#109](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/109)** — macOS
  Developer ID code signing is environment-limited (corporate keychain
  policy). Releases are ad-hoc signed; first-launch quarantine clearance
  is documented in the macOS install instructions above.
- **[#19](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/19)** — `-Dsimd=true`
  build still fails on Windows due to a Zig 0.15 + ghostty-vt C++
  include-path interaction. Default builds (without `-Dsimd`) are
  unaffected.

## Verification

- [ ] macOS: `Ctrl+Cmd+Space` opens the Emoji & Symbols picker;
      `Esc` closes it; `Ctrl+Cmd+Space` again still works as a toggle.
- [ ] macOS: pressing `Esc` while the picker is closed behaves normally
      (passes `\x1b` to the PTY — vim normal mode etc. still work).
- [ ] macOS: pressing `Esc` after the picker has been closed by any
      external path does NOT re-open it (no stale-state surprise).
- [ ] Windows: rebuild boots, F12 toggle works, no regressions vs.
      v0.3.0.
- [ ] DMG mounts on Apple Silicon and Intel, both slices present
      (`lipo -info /Applications/TildaZ.app/Contents/MacOS/tildaz`).
